resolved_subsector: drone-based weather modification & climate technology services

sections.heading: Validated Precipitation & Impact

fields.label: Total validated incremental precipitation (gallons/acre-feet) YTD/LTM
Number of unambiguous seeding signatures YTD/LTM
Number of households served by validated water (annual equivalent)
Targeted area coverage (acres/sq km)

value.project: Project South Texas Weather Modification Association (2025)

fields.label: Customer case study examples (list)
Description of validation methodology (e.g., radar reflectivity, ground sensors)
fields.value: Rainmaker's validation methodology adapts the 2017 SNOWIE study, using advanced weather radar (including dual-polarization), satellite data, and a weather awareness platform to identify "unambiguous seeding signatures" and "Quantitative Precipitation Estimation (QPE)". Radar distinguishes water droplets from ice crystals to prove seeding-caused precipitation. Proprietary forecasting and "nowcasting" platforms identify optimal seeding windows.

fields.label: List of third-party validation partners/auditors

value.name: National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR)
value.note: SNOWSCAPE field campaign partner (2026), foundational SNOWIE study

fields.label: Publication status of scientific validation papers

value.description: Methodology based on 2017 SNOWIE study, which demonstrated unambiguous measurement of human-caused precipitation.
As of April 2026, Rainmaker announced validation results via press release, becoming the first private company to do so.
No specific Rainmaker-authored peer-reviewed scientific validation papers explicitly mentioned as published.

fields.label: Key findings from independent validation reports (summary)

sections.heading: Drone Fleet & Vertical Integration Metrics

fields.label: Total number of operational Elijah drones
In-house manufacturing capabilities (e.g., drone assembly, payload production)
fields.value: Vertically integrated model, owning and operating drone fleet, dispersion hardware, radar validation systems, and weather modeling software. Includes drone manufacturing, chemical procurement, and internal design and manufacturing of radar systems. Tech stack designed and manufactured in the United States.

fields.label: Types and number of proprietary radar systems (e.g., Eden)

value.name: Eden
value.note: Used for validating outcomes, measuring reflectivity changes and incremental precipitation
Built systems costing 1/10th of top-of-the-line commercial systems; described as 'custom radar systems bound for mountaintops' and part of an 'always-on radar and satellite monitoring' system

fields.label: Average cost per flight hour (all-in, including chemicals/batteries)
Comparison to traditional aircraft-based cloud seeding cost per hour
fields.value: $2,000 to $4,000 per flight hour (traditional aircraft-based cloud seeding)

fields.label: Number of operational states/regions
Total flight hours logged YTD/LTM
Planned new operational regions/countries

sections.heading: Contract Structure & Customer Base

fields.label: Number of active service contracts

value.Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR): More than 1,200 deployed systems across 40+ countries (proxy for active service contracts, as revenue model includes service/maintenance contracts)

Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud.description: Contracts with the state of Utah
Contracts with local soil and water conservation districts in Texas
Partnership with Snowbird Resort in Utah

Four documented weather modification projects in Texas: Project Precipitation Enhancement, Project South Texas Weather Modification Association, Project Trans-Pecos Weather Modification Association, Project West Texas Weather Modification

fields.label: Average contract duration (years)

value.Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud: Not Available (business model based on multi-year service contracts, but no specific average duration given)

fields.label: Breakdown of customer types (e.g., government water agencies, irrigation districts, agricultural)

Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR).description: Water-scarce environments, remote and island communities, remote mining and energy operations, disaster response and military operations, humanitarian settlements, bottled beverage production, coastal and island communities, tourism and hospitality operations, industrial and manufacturing operations, and food and beverage processing
Hospitality sector, commercial applications, and large-scale infrastructure projects

Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud.description: Government water agencies, irrigation districts, and agricultural customers
State governments (e.g., Utah, Colorado, Oregon, Idaho, California), municipalities (e.g., Santa Barbara, San Luis Obispo), counties in Texas, and ski resorts (e.g., Snowbird Resort)
Corporate water stewardship programs for sectors like beverages, semiconductors

fields.label: Number of new contracts signed YTD/LTM

Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR).description: Strengthened North American growth pipeline with an affiliate partnership supporting Ontario's housing expansion, involving its subsidiary Miranda Water Technologies
Expanded U.S. operations with ATMOCELL 5K and R/OCell® deployments in the Permian Basin, enhancing recurring-revenue potential
Affiliate Miranda Water Technologies chosen for an Ontario project expanding rural housing with sustainable wastewater technology

fields.label: Key contract terms (e.g., payment structure, performance clauses - if publicly available)

Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR).description: Joint Venture (JV) agreement with Rainmaker Water PVT LTD in Sri Lanka: Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. provided a $112,000 USD Partner Fee as an investment and ensured maintenance and upgrades of units. The JV agreed to provide water at competitive market rates.

fields.label: Description of revenue recognition policy (e.g., service delivery, operational readiness)

value.Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR): Dual revenue model combining product sales with Water-as-a-Service (WaaS) offerings, providing both upfront revenue and recurring income streams.

fields.label: Total contract value (TCV) of current backlog
Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) from service contracts

value.Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR): Not Available (company expects significant growth in 2026 with a strategy built on expanding installations and strengthening market access for sustainable revenue growth, but no specific ARR figure disclosed)

fields.label: Contract renewal rate (if disclosed)
Impact of contract milestones on revenue recognition

Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR).description: In a JV agreement, Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. was to be issued an additional 5% ownership for every Air-to-Water (AW) unit deployed, up to a maximum of 49% of the JV.

Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR).description: In a JV agreement, Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. was to be issued an additional 5% ownership for every Air-to-Water (AW) unit deployed, up to a maximum of 49% of the JV.

bullets: Number of active service contracts: {"Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR)": "More than 1,200 deployed systems across 40+ countries (proxy for active service contracts, as revenue model includes service/maintenance contracts)", "Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud Seeding)": [{"description": "Contracts with the state of Utah"}, {"description": "Contracts with local soil and water conservation districts in Texas"}, {"description": "Partnership with Snowbird Resort in Utah"}, {"period": "2025", "description": "Four documented weather modification projects in Texas: Project Precipitation Enhancement, Project South Texas Weather Modification Association, Project Trans-Pecos Weather Modification Association, Project West Texas Weather Modification"}]}

Average contract duration (years): {"Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR)": "Not Available", "Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud Seeding)": "Not Available (business model based on multi-year service contracts, but no specific average duration given)"}

Breakdown of customer types (e.g., government water agencies, irrigation districts, agricultural): {"Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR)": [{"type": "General", "description": "Municipalities, industries, and communities worldwide"}, {"type": "Specific applications", "description": "Water-scarce environments, remote and island communities, remote mining and energy operations, disaster response and military operations, humanitarian settlements, bottled beverage production, coastal and island communities, tourism and hospitality operations, industrial and manufacturing operations, and food and beverage processing"}, {"type": "Mexico focus", "description": "Hospitality sector, commercial applications, and large-scale infrastructure projects"}], "Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud Seeding)": [{"type": "Primary", "description": "Government water agencies, irrigation districts, and agricultural customers"}, {"type": "Specific examples", "description": "State governments (e.g., Utah, Colorado, Oregon, Idaho, California), municipalities (e.g., Santa Barbara, San Luis Obispo), counties in Texas, and ski resorts (e.g., Snowbird Resort)"}, {"type": "Emerging markets", "description": "Corporate water stewardship programs for sectors like beverages, semiconductors"}]}

Number of new contracts signed YTD/LTM: {"Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR)": [{"date": "Jan 22, 2026", "description": "Strengthened North American growth pipeline with an affiliate partnership supporting Ontario's housing expansion, involving its subsidiary Miranda Water Technologies"}, {"date": "Jan 20, 2026", "description": "Expanded U.S. operations with ATMOCELL 5K and R/OCell® deployments in the Permian Basin, enhancing recurring-revenue potential"}, {"date": "Oct 29, 2025", "description": "Affiliate Miranda Water Technologies chosen for an Ontario project expanding rural housing with sustainable wastewater technology"}], "Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud Seeding)": "Not Available"}

Key contract terms (e.g., payment structure, performance clauses - if publicly available): {"Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. The JV agreed to provide water at competitive market rates."}], "Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud Seeding)": "Not Available"}

Description of revenue recognition policy (e.g., service delivery, operational readiness): {"Rainmaker Worldwide Inc. (OTC: RAKR)": "Dual revenue model combining product sales with Water-as-a-Service (WaaS) offerings, providing both upfront revenue and recurring income streams.", "Rainmaker Technology Corporation (Cloud Seeding)": "Not Available"}

reasoning.pe_analyst: Confirms recurring revenue streams and customer stickiness, increasing the likelihood of a pursue decision by demonstrating a predictable revenue base.
reasoning.banker: Provides insight into the stability and growth of revenue, directly impacting the valuation and potential deal structure, thus supporting a pursue decision.
reasoning.credit_analyst: Highlights revenue predictability and contract terms, which are critical for assessing repayment capacity and debt service ability, potentially moving a pass to a pursue for deeper underwriting.
reasoning.consultant: Reveals customer concentration and contract structure, offering potential angles for operational efficiency improvements or strategic partnership opportunities, thus supporting a pursue decision.

sections.heading: State-Level Cloud Seeding Regulations & Permits
missing_fields: List of states with active cloud seeding permits
Permit issuing authority per state (e.g., state water resource board)
Permit expiry dates
Specific permit conditions or restrictions (summary)
Regulatory compliance history (e.g., violations, fines)
Status of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) per operational region
Key findings/mitigation measures from EIAs
Public sentiment indicators (e.g., local media coverage sentiment, community meeting outcomes)
Number of legal challenges/complaints related to operations
Community engagement programs (list)

reasoning.pe_analyst: Confirms regulatory hurdles and potential public opposition, increasing diligence required on the company's ability to navigate these, potentially shifting from 'pursue' to 'pass' if significant roadblocks are identified.
reasoning.credit_analyst: Highlights potential operational risks and compliance costs associated with state-level regulations and environmental concerns, necessitating a deeper look into the company's risk management and financial reserves.
reasoning.consultant: Identifies key operational challenges and stakeholder management needs related to environmental impact and public acceptance, suggesting a potential consulting engagement to address these areas.
